titlestmt.titleproper: Jerry E. and Norma J. Stillwell papers2621
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Jerry E. and Norma J. Stillwell papers
did.physdesc: Correspondence, printed material, publications, research materials.
did.abstract: Letters, diaries, and notes concerning the recording of bird calls, and other ornithology related work.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Letters, diaries, and notes concerning the recording of bird calls, begun after Jerry Stillwell's retirement (1948) as a technical editor for the American Petroleum Institute in Dallas, Texas, including extensive correspondence with Peter Paul Kellogg and William Fish, both of the Cornell University Laboratory of Ornithology, which later resulted in their records, "Bird Songs of Door Yard, Field, and Forest" (three albums); manuscripts, typescripts, and correspondence relating to the publication of their articles in NATURE and other naturalists' magazines, and Norma Stillwell's book BIRD SONGS: THE ADVENTURE AND TECHNIQUES IN RECORDING THE SONGS OF AMERICAN BIRDS (Garden City, New York: Doubleday, 1964); notebooks (some supplemented by snapshots) and journals of numerous field trips to the Ozarks, Carson National Forest, New Mexico, and other places around the country, particularly in the Midwest and Southwest, which contain observations on birds, trees, and wildflowers; also included are newspaper clippings and other printed material on ornithology and bioacoustics.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series II. Birdsong Files: Journals, Manuscripts, and Research Boxes 2-4

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Jerry E. and Norma J. Stillwell papers, #2621. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Ornithologists.
Ornithology -- United States.
Birds -- United States.
Bird-song -- Recording and reproducing.
Bioacoustics.
controlaccess.title: Bird songs: the adventure and techniques in recording the songs of the American birds.

did.unittitle: Series II. Birdsong Files: Journals, Manuscripts and Research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook of birds recorded and 1 typewritten with notes on identification by bird types

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes on birdsong written in musical notation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For Texas Ornithological Society

did.unittitle: "Rambles of a Pair of Bird Recorders"

scopecontent.p: Chapter 2 "The Valley" and Chapter 3 "Few Birds, But Friendly Folks"

scopecontent.p: Chapter 4 "Kelloggs and the Everglades" and two discarded sections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chapter 5 "Otter Creek Park", Chapter 6 "Avian Echoes" and 1 discarded section

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chapter 7 "Year of the Hermit Thrush", Chapter 8 "We Go West", Cahpter 9 "Singing Stars and Snowy Vistas" last page corresponds to last page of Chapter 10 "The Birds Give an Encore"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chapter 10 "The Birds Give an Encore" and miscellaneous draft chapters
